error TS-1: Pre-emit (9) and post-emit (10) diagnostic counts do not match! This can indicate that a semantic _error_ was added by the emit resolver - such an error may not be reflected on the command line or in the editor, but may be captured in a baseline here!

shorthandPropertyAssignmentsInDestructuring.ts(111,14): error TS1312: Did you mean to use a ':'? An '=' can only follow a property name when the containing object literal is part of a destructuring pattern.


!!! error TS-1: Pre-emit (9) and post-emit (10) diagnostic counts do not match! This can indicate that a semantic _error_ was added by the emit resolver - such an error may not be reflected on the command line or in the editor, but may be captured in a baseline here!
!!! related TS-1: The excess diagnostics are:
!!! related TS18004 shorthandPropertyAssignmentsInDestructuring.ts:111:12: No value exists in scope for the shorthand property 's'. Either declare one or provide an initializer.
==== shorthandPropertyAssignmentsInDestructuring.ts (9 errors) ====
    (function() {
    	var s0;
    	for ({ s0 = 5 } of [{ s0: 1 }]) {
    	}
    });
    (function() {
    	var s0;
    	for ({ s0:s0 = 5 } of [{ s0: 1 }]) {
    	}
    });
    
    (function() {
    	var s1;
    	for ({ s1 = 5 } of [{}]) {
    	}
    });
    
    (function() {
    	var s1;
    	for ({ s1:s1 = 5 } of [{}]) {
    	}
    });
    
    (function() {
    	var s2;
    	for ({ s2 = 5 } of [{ s2: "" }]) {
    	}
    });
    
    (function() {
    	var s2;
    	for ({ s2:s2 = 5 } of [{ s2: "" }]) {
    	}
    });

    	var s3: string;
    	for ({ s3 = 5 } of [{ s3: "" }]) {
    	       ~~

    });

    	var s3: string;
    	for ({ s3:s3 = 5 } of [{ s3: "" }]) {

!!! error TS2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.

    });
    
    (function() {
    	let y;
    	({ y = 5 } = { y: 1 })
    });
    
    (function() {
    	let y;
    	({ y:y = 5 } = { y: 1 })
    });
    
    (function() {
    	let y0: number;
    	({ y0 = 5 } = { y0: 1 })
    });
    
    (function() {
    	let y0: number;
    	({ y0:y0 = 5 } = { y0: 1 })
    });

    	let y2: string, y3: { x: string };

!!! error TS2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
!!! related TS6500 shorthandPropertyAssignmentsInDestructuring.ts:84:24: The expected type comes from property 'x' which is declared here on type '{ x: string; }'
    });
    
    (function() {
    	let y4: number, y5: { x: number };
    	({ y4 = 5, y5 = { x: 1 } } = {})
    });
    
    (function() {
    	let y4: number, y5: { x: number };
    	({ y4:y4 = 5, y5:y5 = { x: 1 } } = {})
    });
    
    
    (function() {
    	let z;
    	({ z = { x: 5 } } = { z: { x: 1 } });
    });
    
    
    (function() {
    	let z;
    	({ z:z = { x: 5 } } = { z: { x: 1 } });
    });
    
    (function() {
    	let a = { s = 5 };
    	            ~
!!! error TS1312: Did you mean to use a ':'? An '=' can only follow a property name when the containing object literal is part of a destructuring pattern.
    });
    
    function foo({a = 4, b = { x: 5 }}) {
    }
